text_block: Cornelius Vanderbilt liquidated his entire shipping empire at his peak to bet on railroads, proving that <yellow>redeploying capital</yellow> into emerging infrastructure yields the greatest <red>generational wealth</red>.

yt_search_query: Bank of Saint George Genoa history
text_block: The Bank of Saint George in Genoa managed state debt in the fifteenth century, proving that <yellow>compounding interest</yellow> on secure bonds could fund a republic for <red>four centuries</red>.
